title: passifloraceae
intro: The Passifloraceae rank, also known as the Passion Flower family, is a taxonomic rank that refers to a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. This rank is a family classification, consisting of approximately 569 species divided into 80 genera. The Passifloraceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with some species cultivated for their ornamental value and edible fruits. Members of this family are known for their distinctive flowers with a unique structure, often featuring a combination of showy corona and androecium.
